_meta.note: Edit this file to change what Vantage shows. The app loads it at startup when served over http. Building stats, availabilities, and tenant rosters here are SAMPLE/illustrative until replaced with verified data from clean sources (firsthand broker knowledge, public records, leasing packages, company sites). Never CoStar's database.
_meta.fields: buildings[].amen = [name, category, lat, lng, rating, blurb]. category must be one of categories[].key. categories[] = {key,label,color,weighted}; weighted:true categories are scored + get a priority slider, weighted:false are display-only map layers. buildings[].access = optional {label: value} strings shown in the dossier left rail (manually entered; not auto-calculated; hidden if absent). rosters keyed by building id = [company, industry]. industries[] = allowed company types (display order).

buildings.name: The Water Garden
buildings.read: A tight, walkable pocket strong on specialty coffee, with a full health club and sauna inside the campus and TRIFIT a three-minute walk. The honest gap is sit-down dinner — the standout restaurants cluster down by the pier, well past a ten-minute walk, so evenings mean a short drive. Best for a team that values a coffee-and-gym daytime rhythm over a dense dining scene.

buildings.name: Clock Tower Building
buildings.read: The densest option by a wide margin. Wall-to-wall dining off the Third Street Promenade, multiple gyms including Equinox, and excellent coffee within a couple of blocks — a tenant here can walk to nearly everything. The tradeoff is downtown bustle and pricier parking, and the energy skews tourist-and-retail rather than quiet professional.

buildings.name: Santa Monica Business Park
buildings.read: Quieter and more self-contained, with a genuinely good little Ocean Park food cluster right at the door — Crimson, Vito, Il Forno, Pontu, and Society Kitchen are all a few minutes away. A 24 Hour Fitness with a sauna and two boutique studios sit close, parking is easy, and the airport-adjacent calm suits a heads-down team. Less coffee density and no Promenade buzz, which is the point for some tenants.

buildings.name: Lantana
buildings.read: Media-campus calm next to Bergamot Station. The catch is that almost nothing sits immediately at the door — the nearest cluster is a six-to-seven minute walk south to the Pico strip, where Rae's, The Brixton, and The Upper West anchor a solid run, with Intelligentsia for coffee and Spry for training. It rewards a team that drives in or values a quiet, design-forward base over walk-out density.
